task.title: How to Go Trick-or-Treating
task.shortdesc: Trick-or-treating is a Halloween activity in which children visit homes in their neighborhood to collect candy and other treats.

prereq.p: Before you begin, make sure you have a costume, a bag or container for collecting candy, and adult supervision if required.

info.p: Choose a costume that is comfortable, visible at night, and appropriate for the weather.

step.cmd: Visit a nearby house with porch lights turned on.

info.p: Homes with lights on typically indicate that the residents are participating in trick-or-treating.

step.cmd: Ring the doorbell or knock on the door.
Say “Trick or treat!” when the door is opened.
Accept the treat politely and say “thank you.”
Move on to the next house.

result.p: You will have collected candy or other treats while participating in a traditional Halloween activity.
It is possible that you will have collected Candy Corn.
